item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this one I like to think I am moderately environmentally consensus but I don't tend to speak out as much as I should. This is a beautiful way of speaking out and unique in its own right. Not to say there haven't been environmental SCPs but this one is nice in its simplicity and the beauty of the bird. The mans Journal was a nice touch and really put a punch to the end that was great. Like a fine wine with the steak of the main scp but not to complicated. I see some people complaining that it isn't subtle enough of suspenseful but that is not the point of this scp in my opinion. This is simply a yell into the darkness to get out what the author is thinking. Definitely a +1

De Mortuis Nihil Nisi Bonum

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
honestly, the only narrative here is the note at the bottom. it's kind of short, and it just puts the SCP out there for the reader. no redaction for suspense, either. its a thing that does a thing. sorry, but -1.

(sorry if i sounded mean >~<)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beautifully tragic and painfully to the point. This SCP wasn’t scary because it’s a threat, but because it’s horrifyingly real, and the striking portrait that the final addendum paints is haunting. Unique and powerful, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This must be the saddest SCP I've ever read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've actually linked a recording of the real bird's song in my author comment if you want to listen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"And these ghosts will sing. They don't know anything else other than to just sing as they would in any other time, not knowing why they will never receive a song in return. An unanswered call, that will last until the Earth is ready to snap its own fingers, and let them go too into the void."
God I just want to hear this call. I nearly can hear it just from these words alone.
Thanks for the birdsong, friend.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"And these ghosts will sing. They don't know anything else other than to just sing as they would in any other time, not knowing why they will never receive a song in return."

Haunting, provocative, and very well written. This is a great example of a simple anomaly explored in a simple way, but skillfully crafted and refined, made all the more potent by the way it's expressed what is left unsaid.

If I was to bring up anything, it'd be that the closing addendum verges on being long winded and waxing. This can be explained away by the subject genuinely spilling their guts out onto the page, and the effect the anomaly has caused, but I'd be amiss writing a response without some kind of criticism.

Either way, great work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No need to be sorry, I have no problem with disagreement! And judging by the rating, plenty of people disagree with me and agree with you.

It doesn't change my personal feelings, it still feels very ham-fisted to me, but congrats to Sterbai on making a solid skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, I'm sorry, but I disagree with you strongly. It does explain it, but it is done not only well but in a way that conveys the idea to the reader that improves upon what is already expressed within the article itself. The message is made clear, but not shoved down the throat.

An explanation in of itself isn't wrong, and this idea that everything HAS to be subtle is, in my opinion, silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, original, and interesting, plus that great addendum, this is a great S.C.P. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like the way that it seems like a completely mundane and boring SCP until you read that Addendum. Realizing the way that the phenomenon is nowhere near as interesting as the way it affected him. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I heard a lonesome call and was inspired

This is a short skip about extinction and the impact that it has on the world, and the people who care. More of an experiment with writing a very short-form article.

Image source https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kauaioo.jpg (public domain image)

Thanks to -

rumetzen
Uncle Nicolini
Rounderhouse
The Great Hippo
Modulum

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept, but I don't like that the note just explains the concept straight to the reader. If you could find a more subtle way to communicate the idea, I'd happily change my downvote to an upvote!
